title: Risk analysis and information system security policies
description: Establish and maintain policies on risk analysis and information system security covering all space and ground segment assets.
evidenceGuidance: Documented risk assessment methodology, asset inventory with classification, risk register with treatment plans, approved security policies, annual review records.
applicabilityNotes: For space operators: must cover satellite bus systems, ground station networks, TT&C links, and mission control infrastructure. Consider RF-specific risks (jamming, spoofing) alongside traditional IT risks.

title: Space asset classification and criticality assessment
description: Classify all space assets by criticality to service delivery and assess their exposure to cyber threats across the full mission lifecycle.
evidenceGuidance: Asset register with criticality ratings, dependency mapping between space and ground segments, threat exposure assessment per asset class.
applicabilityNotes: Map dependencies between orbital assets and ground infrastructure. A compromised ground station can affect all satellites it controls.

title: Incident handling procedures
description: Implement procedures for detecting, managing, and resolving cybersecurity incidents affecting space infrastructure.
evidenceGuidance: Incident response plan, defined severity levels, escalation procedures, roles and responsibilities matrix, incident log with timestamps.
applicabilityNotes: Space-specific incidents include telemetry anomalies, unauthorized telecommands, RF interference events, and ground segment intrusions.

title: Incident notification to authorities
description: Notify the competent authority or CSIRT of significant incidents: early warning within 24 hours, incident notification within 72 hours, final report within one month.
evidenceGuidance: Notification templates, submission records with timestamps, evidence of meeting 24h/72h/1month deadlines, designated point of contact.
applicabilityNotes: Under the proposed EU Space Act, operators may also need to report to EUSPA within 12 hours. Prepare for dual reporting obligations.

title: Incident detection and monitoring capabilities
description: Deploy technical capabilities to detect cybersecurity incidents in real-time across space and ground infrastructure.
evidenceGuidance: Security monitoring architecture, log sources and retention policy, alerting rules documentation, evidence of monitoring coverage.
applicabilityNotes: Must monitor both IT systems (mission control, data processing) and OT/space-specific systems (TT&C interfaces, telemetry streams, RF link quality).

title: Business continuity and disaster recovery
description: Maintain business continuity plans including backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis management for space operations.
evidenceGuidance: Business continuity plan, disaster recovery procedures, backup schedules and test results, crisis management team roster, annual BCP test records.
applicabilityNotes: Consider satellite redundancy, backup ground station procedures, failover for mission-critical data links, and recovery procedures for compromised spacecraft.

title: Mission continuity under cyber attack
description: Ensure continued mission operations during and after a cybersecurity incident, including safe-mode procedures for affected spacecraft.
evidenceGuidance: Mission degradation procedures, satellite safe-mode activation protocols, backup communication paths, service continuity agreements.
applicabilityNotes: Define procedures for maintaining satellite health when ground control is compromised. Include autonomous satellite operations capability assessment.

title: Supply chain security
description: Assess and manage cybersecurity risks in the supply chain, including relationships with direct suppliers and service providers.
evidenceGuidance: Supplier security assessment records, contractual security requirements, vendor risk register, third-party audit results or certifications.
applicabilityNotes: Assess COTS component suppliers, ground station service providers, launch service cybersecurity, and cloud providers hosting mission data.

title: Ground segment provider security assessment
description: Evaluate the cybersecurity posture of all ground station operators, data relay providers, and ground-segment-as-a-service vendors.
evidenceGuidance: Vendor security questionnaire results, SLAs with security clauses, evidence of periodic reassessment, access control reviews.
applicabilityNotes: Many operators use shared ground station networks. Each provider must be assessed for their ability to protect command uplinks and telemetry downlinks.

title: Security in system acquisition, development, and maintenance
description: Embed security throughout the lifecycle of network and information systems, including vulnerability handling and disclosure.
evidenceGuidance: Secure development lifecycle documentation, code review records, vulnerability scanning reports, patch management policy, responsible disclosure policy.
applicabilityNotes: Includes flight software development, ground segment software, firmware updates to spacecraft (where possible), and mission planning tools.

title: Vulnerability management for space systems
description: Maintain a process for identifying, assessing, and remediating vulnerabilities in space and ground segment systems.
evidenceGuidance: Vulnerability scanning schedule and results, patch deployment records, risk acceptance documentation for unpatched systems, CVE tracking.
applicabilityNotes: Many satellite systems cannot be patched in orbit. Document risk acceptance for known vulnerabilities and compensating controls at the ground segment.

title: Effectiveness assessment of cybersecurity measures
description: Implement policies and procedures to assess whether cybersecurity risk-management measures are effective.
evidenceGuidance: Penetration test reports, security audit results, compliance assessment records, KPI tracking, management review minutes.
applicabilityNotes: Under the proposed EU Space Act, operators must conduct Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) prior to launch and every three years.

title: Cyber hygiene and training
description: Ensure basic cyber hygiene practices and provide cybersecurity training to all personnel.
evidenceGuidance: Training program documentation, attendance records, phishing simulation results, security awareness materials, role-based training records.
applicabilityNotes: Training should cover: recognizing anomalous telemetry, handling satellite command credentials, RF interference awareness, and social engineering defense.

title: Cryptography and encryption policies
description: Establish policies on the use of cryptography and encryption for protecting data in transit and at rest.
evidenceGuidance: Encryption policy, key management procedures, inventory of cryptographic implementations, crypto-agility assessment, certificate management.
applicabilityNotes: Space-specific: TT&C link encryption (SDLS/OTAR), telemetry encryption, ground network encryption. Assess post-quantum cryptography readiness.

title: Space link encryption and key management
description: Implement and maintain encryption for satellite command and telemetry links with appropriate key management procedures.
evidenceGuidance: Link encryption specifications, key generation and distribution procedures, key rotation schedule, emergency key revocation procedures.
applicabilityNotes: SDLS compliance where applicable. Consider key rotation challenges for satellites with limited on-board crypto capability.

title: Human resources security and access control
description: Implement human resources security measures, access control policies, and asset management procedures.
evidenceGuidance: Access control policy, user access reviews, privileged access management records, background check procedures, offboarding checklists.
applicabilityNotes: Privileged access to satellite command systems must be tightly controlled. Implement separation of duties for critical commands.

title: Multi-factor authentication
description: Deploy multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication for access to critical systems.
evidenceGuidance: MFA implementation records, coverage assessment, exception documentation, continuous authentication evaluation.
applicabilityNotes: MFA must cover: mission control access, ground station remote access, satellite command interfaces, and cloud-hosted mission data platforms.

title: Secured emergency communication systems
description: Maintain secured voice, video, text, and emergency communication systems within the entity.
evidenceGuidance: Emergency communication plan, secure communication tools inventory, out-of-band communication procedures, annual test records.
applicabilityNotes: Space operators need out-of-band communication capability in case primary ground networks are compromised.
